heading: Improving uptake and patient access to integrated care services
label: 290bb–42 Improving uptake and patient access to integrated care services
notes: References in TextSection 223 of the Protecting Access to Medicare Act of 2014, referred to in subsec. (g)(2), is , which is set out as a note under .section 223 of Pub. L. 113–93section 1396a of this title

Amendments2022— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section authorized Secretary to award grants and cooperative agreements to eligible entities to support improvement of integrated care for primary care and behavioral health care.Pub. L. 117–3282016— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section related to awards for co-locating primary and specialty care in community-based mental health settings.Pub. L. 114–255

children.label: A
children.text: 1 or more qualified community programs as described in ; orsection 300x–2(b)(1) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: 1 or more health centers (as defined in ), rural health clinics (as defined in ), or Federally qualified health centers (as defined in such section), or primary care practices serving adult or pediatric patients or both.section 254b(a) of this titlesection 1395x(aa) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: The term “integrated care” means collaborative models, including the psychiatric collaborative care model and other evidence-based or evidence-informed models, or practices for coordinating and jointly delivering behavioral and physical health services, which may include practices that share the same space in the same facility.

children.label: B
children.text: The term “bidirectional integrated care” means the integration of behavioral health care and specialty physical health care, and the integration of primary and physical health care within specialty behavioral health settings, including within primary health care settings.

children.label: A
children.text: care directed by the primary care team;

children.label: B
children.text: structured care management;

children.label: C
children.text: regular assessments of clinical status using developmentally appropriate, validated tools; and

children.label: D
children.text: modification of treatment as appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: adults with a serious mental illness or adults who have co-occurring mental illness and physical health conditions or chronic disease;

children.label: B
children.text: children and adolescents with a serious emotional disturbance who have a co-occurring physical health condition or chronic disease;

children.label: C
children.text: individuals with a substance use disorder; or

children.label: D
children.text: individuals with a mental illness who have a co-occurring substance use disorder.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may award grants and cooperative agreements to eligible entities to support the improvement of integrated care for physical and behavioral health care in accordance with paragraph (2).

children.label: A
children.text: to promote full integration and collaboration in clinical practices between physical and behavioral health care, including for special populations;

children.label: B
children.text: to support the improvement of integrated care models for physical and behavioral health care to improve overall wellness and physical health status, including for special populations;

children.label: C
children.text: to promote the implementation and improvement of bidirectional integrated care services provided at entities described in subsection (a)(1), including evidence-based or evidence-informed screening, assessment, diagnosis, prevention, treatment, and recovery services for mental and substance use disorders, and co-occurring physical health conditions and chronic diseases; and

children.label: i
children.text: by hiring staff;

children.label: ii
children.text: by identifying and formalizing contractual relationships with other health care providers or other relevant entities offering care management and behavioral health consultation to facilitate the adoption of integrated care, including, as applicable, providers who will function as psychiatric consultants and behavioral health care managers in providing behavioral health integration services through the collaborative care model;

children.label: iii
children.text: by purchasing or upgrading software and other resources, as applicable, needed to appropriately provide behavioral health integration, including resources needed to establish a patient registry and implement measurement-based care; and

children.label: iv
children.text: for such other purposes as the Secretary determines to be applicable and appropriate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An eligible entity that is seeking a grant or cooperative agreement under this section shall submit an application to the Secretary at such time, in such manner, and accompanied by such information as the Secretary may require, including the contents described in paragraph (2).

children.label: A
children.text: a description of a plan to achieve fully collaborative agreements to provide bidirectional integrated care to special populations;

children.label: B
children.text: a summary of the policies, if any, that are barriers to the provision of integrated care, and the specific steps, if applicable, that will be taken to address such barriers;

children.label: C
children.text: a description of partnerships or other arrangements with local health care providers to provide services to special populations and, as applicable, in areas with demonstrated need, such as Tribal, rural, or other medically underserved communities, such as those with a workforce shortage of mental health and substance use disorder, pediatric mental health, or other related professionals;

children.label: D
children.text: an agreement and plan to report to the Secretary performance measures necessary to evaluate patient outcomes and facilitate evaluations across participating projects; and

children.label: E
children.text: a description of the plan or progress in implementing the psychiatric collaborative care model, as applicable and appropriate;

children.label: F
children.text: a description of the plan or progress of evidence-based or evidence-informed integrated care models other than the psychiatric collaborative care model implemented by primary care practices, as applicable and appropriate; and

children.label: G
children.text: a plan for sustainability beyond the grant or cooperative agreement period under subsection (e).

children.heading: Grant and cooperative agreement amounts
children.label: d Grant and cooperative agreement amounts

children.heading: Target amount
children.label: 1 Target amount
children.text: The target amount that an eligible entity may receive for a year through a grant or cooperative agreement under this section shall be no more than $2,000,000.

children.heading: Adjustment permitted
children.label: 2 Adjustment permitted
children.text: The Secretary, taking into consideration the quality of an eligible entity’s application and the number of eligible entities that received grants under this section prior to , may adjust the target amount that an eligible entity may receive for a year through a grant or cooperative agreement under this section.December 29, 2022

children.label: A
children.text: may not allocate more than 10 percent of the funds awarded to such eligible entity under this section to administrative functions; and

children.label: B
children.text: shall allocate the remainder of such funding to health facilities that provide integrated care.

children.heading: Duration
children.label: e Duration
children.text: A grant or cooperative agreement under this section shall be for a period not to exceed 5 years.
the progress made to reduce barriers to integrated care as described in the entity’s application under subsection (c);

a description of outcomes with respect to each special population listed in subsection (a)(4), including outcomes related to education, employment, and housing, or, as applicable and appropriate, outcomes for such populations receiving behavioral health care through the psychiatric collaborative care model in primary care practices; and
progress in meeting performance metrics and other relevant benchmarks; and
such other information that the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Technical assistance for primary-behavioral health care integration
children.label: g Technical assistance for primary-behavioral health care integration
A
children.text: development and selection of integrated care models;

children.label: B
children.text: dissemination of evidence-based interventions in integrated care;

children.label: C
children.text: establishment of organizational practices to support operational and administrative success; and

children.label: D
children.text: as appropriate, appropriate information, training, and technical assistance in implementing the psychiatric collaborative care model when an eligible entity is collaborating with 1 or more primary care practices for the purposes of implementing the psychiatric collaborative care model.

children.heading: Additional dissemination of technical information
children.label: 2 Additional dissemination of technical information
children.text: In addition to providing the assistance described in paragraph (1) to recipients of a grant or cooperative agreement under this section, the Secretary may also provide such assistance to other States and political subdivisions of States, Indian Tribes and Tribal organizations, as those terms are defined in , outpatient mental health and addiction treatment centers, community mental health centers that meet the criteria under , certified community behavioral health clinics described in section 223 of the Protecting Access to Medicare Act of 2014, primary care organizations such as Federally qualified health centers or rural health clinics as defined in , primary health care practices, the community-based organizations, and other entities engaging in integrated care activities, as the Secretary determines appropriate.section 5304 of title 25section 300x–2(c) of this titlesection 1395x(aa) of this title

children.heading: Report to Congress
children.label: h Report to Congress
children.text: Not later than 18 months after , and annually thereafter, the Secretary shall submit a report to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives summarizing the information submitted in reports to the Secretary under subsection (f), including progress made in meeting performance metrics and the uptake of integrated care models, any adjustments made to target amounts pursuant to subsection (d)(2), and any other relevant information.December 29, 2022

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 1 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: To carry out this section, there is authorized to be appropriated $60,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2023 through 2027.

children.heading: Increasing uptake of the psychiatric collaborative care model by primary care practices
children.label: 2 Increasing uptake of the psychiatric collaborative care model by primary care practices
children.text: Not less than 10 percent of funds appropriated to carry out this section shall be for the purposes of implementing the psychiatric collaborative care model implemented by primary care practices under subsection (b).

children.heading: Funding contingency
children.label: 3 Funding contingency
children.text: Paragraph (2) shall not apply to a fiscal year unless the amount made available to carry out this section for such fiscal year exceeds the amount appropriated to carry out this section (as in effect before ) for fiscal year 2022.December 29, 2022
